mainEntity.name: How long do I have to file a claim after getting hit by falling debris on a construction site?

acceptedAnswer.text: Deadlines vary by state; Utah requires reporting within 180 days, while Idaho requires notice within 60 days. Filing a formal claim also has specific statutes of limitations, so you should report the incident and seek legal advice immediately to avoid losing your rights.
acceptedAnswer.description:
The timeframe for filing a claim after being struck by an object depends on which state you are in. In Utah, you generally have 180 days to report the injury to your employer, but you must file the formal claim with the Labor Commission within six years. In Idaho, you typically must provide notice to your employer within 60 days and file a claim within one year.

Waiting too long creates a "gap in treatment" or a "late notice" defense for the insurance company, who will argue the injury didn't actually happen at work. This is especially risky with "struck-by" incidents where bruises fade but internal damage persists.

Key factors that influence timing include when the "discovery" of the injury occurred and whether your employer initially paid for any treatment. It is always best to file as soon as symptoms appear. If you are approaching a deadline, legal intervention is necessary to preserve your right to medical care and back pay.
